template: {CHARACTER_0} is studying animal classification. A table groups animals by whether they have wings and whether they have hair. Animal Q is a frog. In which group should {CHARACTER_0} place animal Q?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 380 195" width="380" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="190" y="18" text-anchor="middle" font-size="14" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Animal Classification Table</text>
  <!-- corner -->
  <rect x="10" y="28" width="90" height="40" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/>
  <!-- column headers -->
  <rect x="100" y="28" width="130" height="40" fill="#bfdbfe" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="165" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e40af">No Wings</text>
  <rect x="230" y="28" width="140" height="40" fill="#bfdbfe" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="300" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e40af">Has Wings</text>
  <!-- row headers -->
  <rect x="10" y="68" width="90" height="57" fill="#bfdbfe" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="55" y="93" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e40af">No</text>
  <text x="55" y="110" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e40af">Hair</text>
  <rect x="10" y="125" width="90" height="57" fill="#bfdbfe" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="55" y="150" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e40af">Has</text>
  <text x="55" y="167" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e40af">Hair</text>
  <!-- data cells -->
  <rect x="100" y="68" width="130" height="57" fill="white" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="165" y="103" text-anchor="middle" font-size="32" font-weight="bold" fill="#475569">E</text>
  <rect x="230" y="68" width="140" height="57" fill="white" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="300" y="103" text-anchor="middle" font-size="32" font-weight="bold" fill="#475569">F</text>
  <rect x="100" y="125" width="130" height="57" fill="white" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="165" y="160" text-anchor="middle" font-size="32" font-weight="bold" fill="#475569">G</text>
  <rect x="230" y="125" width="140" height="57" fill="white" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="300" y="160" text-anchor="middle" font-size="32" font-weight="bold" fill="#475569">H</text>
</svg>
options: Group with wings but no hair
Group with no wings and no hair
Group with no wings but has hair
correct_answer: Group with no wings and no hair
explanation: A frog is an amphibian. Amphibians do not have wings and do not have hair.

template: {CHARACTER_0} is comparing two organisms: organism X is a fern and organism Y is a flowering plant. Which one of the following statements about organisms X and Y is correct?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 380 200" width="380" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <!-- Organism X: Fern -->
  <text x="90" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Organism X (Fern)</text>
  <line x1="90" y1="168" x2="90" y2="80" stroke="#166534" stroke-width="2"/>
  <path d="M90,130 Q62,112 46,95" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5" fill="none"/>
  <path d="M90,115 Q118,97 134,80" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5" fill="none"/>
  <path d="M90,100 Q68,85 56,75" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5" fill="none"/>
  <path d="M90,90 Q112,75 124,65" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5" fill="none"/>
  <!-- leaflets left -->
  <ellipse cx="58" cy="110" rx="7" ry="4" fill="#86efac" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="0.8" transform="rotate(-30,58,110)"/>
  <ellipse cx="71" cy="97" rx="7" ry="4" fill="#86efac" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="0.8" transform="rotate(-35,71,97)"/>
  <!-- leaflets right -->
  <ellipse cx="122" cy="89" rx="7" ry="4" fill="#86efac" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="0.8" transform="rotate(30,122,89)"/>
  <ellipse cx="110" cy="102" rx="7" ry="4" fill="#86efac" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="0.8" transform="rotate(35,110,102)"/>
  <text x="90" y="158" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">● spores (no flowers)</text>
  <text x="90" y="172" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Green leaves → photosynthesis</text>
  <!-- divider -->
  <line x1="190" y1="10" x2="190" y2="192" stroke="#e2e8f0" stroke-width="2"/>
  <!-- Organism Y: Flowering plant -->
  <text x="290" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Organism Y (Flowering Plant)</text>
  <line x1="290" y1="168" x2="290" y2="108" stroke="#166534" stroke-width="2"/>
  <ellipse cx="267" cy="148" rx="23" ry="9" fill="#4ade80" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1" transform="rotate(-25,267,148)"/>
  <ellipse cx="313" cy="138" rx="23" ry="9" fill="#4ade80" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1" transform="rotate(25,313,138)"/>
  <!-- petals -->
  <ellipse cx="290" cy="92" rx="6" ry="12" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1"/>
  <ellipse cx="290" cy="92" rx="6" ry="12" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1" transform="rotate(60,290,92)"/>
  <ellipse cx="290" cy="92" rx="6" ry="12" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1" transform="rotate(120,290,92)"/>
  <ellipse cx="290" cy="92" rx="6" ry="12" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1" transform="rotate(180,290,92)"/>
  <ellipse cx="290" cy="92" rx="6" ry="12" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1" transform="rotate(240,290,92)"/>
  <ellipse cx="290" cy="92" rx="6" ry="12" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1" transform="rotate(300,290,92)"/>
  <circle cx="290" cy="92" r="8" fill="#f97316"/>
  <text x="290" y="158" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">● seeds / flowers</text>
  <text x="290" y="172" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Green leaves → photosynthesis</text>
</svg>
options: Only organism Y can trap sunlight.
correct_answer: Both carry out photosynthesis.
explanation: Both ferns and flowering plants are green plants, so they can both trap sunlight to carry out photosynthesis to make their own food. Only ferns reproduce by spores, and only flowering plants produce flowers.

template:
{CHARACTER_0}'s science teacher draws a diagram showing how substances V and W are transported in the human body. Substance V is taken in by system X, and substance W is transported by system Y. Which one of the following correctly identifies V, W, X and Y?
(1) V=digested food, W=oxygen, X=circulatory, Y=respiratory
(2) V=digested food, W=oxygen, X=respiratory, Y=circulatory
(3) V=oxygen, W=digested food, X=circulatory, Y=respiratory
(4) V=oxygen, W=digested food, X=respiratory, Y=circulatory
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 440 195" width="440" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="220" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Transport of Substances in the Human Body</text>
  <!-- Substance V entering System X -->
  <text x="80" y="42" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#0369a1" font-weight="bold">Substance V</text>
  <text x="80" y="54" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#0369a1">(from outside)</text>
  <line x1="80" y1="58" x2="80" y2="72" stroke="#0369a1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="74,72 80,82 86,72" fill="#0369a1"/>
  <!-- System X box -->
  <rect x="20" y="82" width="120" height="65" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2" rx="8"/>
  <text x="80" y="112" text-anchor="middle" font-size="14" font-weight="bold" fill="#1d4ed8">System X</text>
  <text x="80" y="130" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#1d4ed8">(takes in V)</text>
  <!-- Arrow X → Y -->
  <line x1="140" y1="115" x2="193" y2="115" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2"/>
  <polygon points="191,109 201,115 191,121" fill="#475569"/>
  <text x="167" y="108" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">V + W</text>
  <!-- System Y box -->
  <rect x="200" y="82" width="120" height="65" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2" rx="8"/>
  <text x="260" y="112" text-anchor="middle" font-size="14" font-weight="bold" fill="#991b1b">System Y</text>
  <text x="260" y="130" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#991b1b">(distributes W)</text>
  <!-- Arrow Y → body -->
  <line x1="320" y1="115" x2="373" y2="115" stroke="#991b1b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="371,109 381,115 371,121" fill="#991b1b"/>
  <!-- Body cells box -->
  <rect x="383" y="97" width="50" height="35" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#d97706" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="408" y="112" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#92400e">Body</text>
  <text x="408" y="125" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#92400e">cells</text>
  <!-- W label -->
  <text x="350" y="107" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#991b1b">Substance W →</text>
  <!-- Question prompt -->
  <text x="220" y="175" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">Identify V, W, X and Y from the options below.</text>
</svg>
correct_answer: (4) oxygen / digested food / respiratory / circulatory
explanation: The respiratory system (X) takes in oxygen (V), and the circulatory system (Y) transports it along with digested food (W) to the rest of the body.

template:
{CHARACTER_0} is studying the life cycles of the mosquito and the grasshopper. The mosquito undergoes complete metamorphosis — its young (larvae) live in water and look very different from the adult. The grasshopper undergoes incomplete metamorphosis — its young (nymphs) live on land and resemble the adult. Both adult mosquitoes and adult grasshoppers have wings and reproduce by laying eggs.

Which of the following statements are correct for BOTH animals?
A: Adult has wings
B: Young resembles the adult
C: Young lives in the water
D: Adult reproduces by laying eggs
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 500 230" width="500" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <!-- MOSQUITO (complete metamorphosis) -->
  <text x="120" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#0369a1">Mosquito</text>
  <text x="120" y="29" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">(complete metamorphosis)</text>
  <!-- Egg -->
  <ellipse cx="55" cy="90" rx="32" ry="22" fill="#e0f2fe" stroke="#0369a1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="55" y="95" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#0369a1">Egg</text>
  <!-- Arrow Egg→Larva -->
  <line x1="88" y1="84" x2="148" y2="76" stroke="#0369a1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="145,70 155,75 149,83" fill="#0369a1"/>
  <!-- Larva (in water) -->
  <ellipse cx="180" cy="72" rx="32" ry="22" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0369a1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="180" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#0369a1">Larva</text>
  <text x="180" y="84" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">(in water)</text>
  <!-- Arrow Larva→Pupa -->
  <line x1="178" y1="94" x2="164" y2="138" stroke="#0369a1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="158,135 163,147 170,137" fill="#0369a1"/>
  <!-- Pupa -->
  <ellipse cx="155" cy="160" rx="32" ry="22" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0369a1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="155" y="165" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#0369a1">Pupa</text>
  <!-- Arrow Pupa→Adult -->
  <line x1="123" y1="155" x2="88" y2="130" stroke="#0369a1" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="83,133 87,122 94,130" fill="#0369a1"/>
  <!-- Adult -->
  <ellipse cx="58" cy="148" rx="32" ry="22" fill="#7dd3fc" stroke="#0369a1" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="58" y="146" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#0369a1">Adult</text>
  <text x="58" y="160" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">has wings</text>
  <!-- Arrow Adult→Egg (cycle) -->
  <line x1="40" y1="128" x2="42" y2="109" stroke="#0369a1" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,2"/>
  <polygon points="37,110 42,100 47,110" fill="#0369a1"/>

  <!-- DIVIDER -->
  <line x1="248" y1="10" x2="248" y2="210" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="6,3"/>

  <!-- GRASSHOPPER (incomplete metamorphosis) -->
  <text x="380" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Grasshopper</text>
  <text x="380" y="29" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">(incomplete metamorphosis)</text>
  <!-- Egg -->
  <ellipse cx="308" cy="90" rx="32" ry="22" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="308" y="95" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#15803d">Egg</text>
  <!-- Arrow Egg→Nymph -->
  <line x1="340" y1="84" x2="398" y2="76" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="395,70 405,75 399,83" fill="#15803d"/>
  <!-- Nymph (on land) -->
  <ellipse cx="432" cy="72" rx="32" ry="22" fill="#bbf7d0" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="432" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#15803d">Nymph</text>
  <text x="432" y="84" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">(on land)</text>
  <!-- Arrow Nymph→Adult -->
  <line x1="416" y1="93" x2="400" y2="138" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="393,136 399,148 406,138" fill="#15803d"/>
  <!-- Adult -->
  <ellipse cx="383" cy="160" rx="38" ry="22" fill="#86efac" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="383" y="158" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Adult</text>
  <text x="383" y="172" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">has wings</text>
  <!-- Arrow Adult→Egg (cycle) -->
  <line x1="348" y1="153" x2="322" y2="112" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,2"/>
  <polygon points="316,114 321,103 328,111" fill="#15803d"/>
</svg>
correct_answer: A and D only
explanation: For both the mosquito and the grasshopper, the adult stage has wings (A) and reproduces by laying eggs (D). However, only the mosquito's young live in water (C), and only the grasshopper's young resemble the adult (B).

template:
{CHARACTER_0} is studying an ocean food web where: krill is eaten by squids and blue whales; squids are eaten by sperm whales, penguins, and killer whales; penguins are eaten by killer whales.

Which of the following would likely cause an INCREASE in the population of squids?
A: A decrease in the population of killer whales
B: A decrease in the population of blue whales
C: A decrease in the population of sperm whales
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 520 290" width="520" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="260" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Ocean Food Web</text>
  <text x="260" y="30" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Arrows show direction of energy: prey → predator</text>
  <!-- KRILL (bottom centre) -->
  <rect x="210" y="240" width="100" height="34" rx="6" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#d97706" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="260" y="262" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#92400e">Krill</text>
  <!-- BLUE WHALE (bottom right) -->
  <rect x="380" y="240" width="120" height="34" rx="6" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="440" y="262" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" fill="#1d4ed8">Blue Whale</text>
  <!-- SQUID (middle centre) -->
  <rect x="210" y="155" width="100" height="34" rx="6" fill="#e0f2fe" stroke="#0284c7" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="260" y="177" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#075985">Squid</text>
  <!-- SPERM WHALE (top left) -->
  <rect x="10" y="60" width="120" height="34" rx="6" fill="#fce7f3" stroke="#be185d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="70" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" fill="#9d174d">Sperm Whale</text>
  <!-- PENGUIN (top centre) -->
  <rect x="200" y="60" width="90" height="34" rx="6" fill="#fce7f3" stroke="#be185d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="245" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" fill="#9d174d">Penguin</text>
  <!-- KILLER WHALE (top right) -->
  <rect x="360" y="60" width="130" height="34" rx="6" fill="#ede9fe" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="425" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" fill="#6d28d9">Killer Whale</text>
  <!-- krill → squid -->
  <line x1="260" y1="240" x2="260" y2="191" stroke="#0284c7" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="254,192 260,181 266,192" fill="#0284c7"/>
  <!-- krill → blue whale -->
  <line x1="310" y1="254" x2="378" y2="256" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="376,250 388,256 376,262" fill="#3b82f6"/>
  <!-- squid → sperm whale -->
  <line x1="215" y1="162" x2="134" y2="88" stroke="#9d174d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="127,92 133,81 141,89" fill="#9d174d"/>
  <!-- squid → penguin -->
  <line x1="247" y1="155" x2="246" y2="96" stroke="#9d174d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="240,97 246,86 252,97" fill="#9d174d"/>
  <!-- squid → killer whale -->
  <line x1="304" y1="163" x2="367" y2="90" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="360,93 369,83 374,93" fill="#7c3aed"/>
  <!-- penguin → killer whale -->
  <line x1="290" y1="77" x2="358" y2="77" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="356,71 368,77 356,83" fill="#7c3aed"/>
</svg>
correct_answer: A and C only
explanation: A: Fewer killer whales means fewer squids eaten directly, so squid population increases. C: Fewer sperm whales means fewer squids eaten, so squid population increases. B: Fewer blue whales means more krill available, but this leads to more squid food competition rather than a direct increase in squid numbers in this food web.

template: {CHARACTER_0} removed two parts from flower P, then transferred pollen from another flower of the same plant onto the remaining stigma. After some time, a fruit was formed. A flower has these parts: A (ovary), B (anthers), C (stigma), D (petals). Which two parts were removed from flower P?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 360 270" width="360" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="180" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Flower (cross-section)</text>
  <!-- Petals (D) – 5 petals -->
  <ellipse cx="180" cy="130" rx="26" ry="52" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" transform="rotate(-72,180,140)"/>
  <ellipse cx="180" cy="130" rx="26" ry="52" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" transform="rotate(-36,180,140)"/>
  <ellipse cx="180" cy="130" rx="26" ry="52" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" transform="rotate(0,180,140)"/>
  <ellipse cx="180" cy="130" rx="26" ry="52" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" transform="rotate(36,180,140)"/>
  <ellipse cx="180" cy="130" rx="26" ry="52" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" transform="rotate(72,180,140)"/>
  <!-- Stem -->
  <rect x="174" y="218" width="12" height="38" fill="#86efac" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1"/>
  <!-- Ovary (A) -->
  <ellipse cx="180" cy="210" rx="20" ry="14" fill="#bbf7d0" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="180" y="214" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">A</text>
  <!-- Style -->
  <rect x="177" y="175" width="6" height="35" fill="#86efac" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1"/>
  <!-- Stigma (C) -->
  <ellipse cx="180" cy="172" rx="13" ry="7" fill="#4ade80" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="180" y="176" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">C</text>
  <!-- Stamens/Anthers (B) – 4 stamens -->
  <line x1="152" y1="195" x2="147" y2="165" stroke="#a16207" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <ellipse cx="146" cy="162" rx="8" ry="5" fill="#fbbf24" stroke="#a16207" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <line x1="162" y1="188" x2="154" y2="158" stroke="#a16207" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <ellipse cx="153" cy="155" rx="8" ry="5" fill="#fbbf24" stroke="#a16207" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <line x1="208" y1="188" x2="206" y2="158" stroke="#a16207" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <ellipse cx="207" cy="155" rx="8" ry="5" fill="#fbbf24" stroke="#a16207" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <line x1="218" y1="195" x2="213" y2="165" stroke="#a16207" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <ellipse cx="214" cy="162" rx="8" ry="5" fill="#fbbf24" stroke="#a16207" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <!-- B label -->
  <text x="232" y="157" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#a16207">B</text>
  <!-- Labels with dashed lines -->
  <line x1="130" y1="172" x2="166" y2="172" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3,2"/>
  <text x="10" y="168" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">C – stigma</text>
  <line x1="118" y1="212" x2="159" y2="210" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3,2"/>
  <text x="10" y="215" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">A – ovary</text>
  <text x="236" y="168" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#a16207">B – anthers</text>
  <line x1="234" y1="106" x2="200" y2="118" stroke="#d97706" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3,2"/>
  <text x="234" y="103" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#d97706">D – petals</text>
</svg>
correct_answer: B and D
explanation: A fruit forms from the ovary (A) after fertilization via the stigma (C). Parts B (anthers) and D (petals) can be removed because the flower can still receive pollen on its stigma from another flower to fertilize the ovules in the ovary.

template:
{CHARACTER_0} is investigating photosynthesis using a plant with leaves that have green and white areas. {CHARACTER_0} kept the plant in the dark for two days to remove all starch, then covered part of a leaf with black paper and left it in sunlight for one day before testing for starch.

The leaf areas are: P (green, uncovered), Q (white, uncovered), R (white, covered), S (green, covered). In which area(s) is starch mostly found?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 270" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Variegated Leaf with Black Paper</text>
  <!-- Leaf base shape background (two halves) -->
  <!-- Green left half -->
  <rect x="70" y="35" width="100" height="190" fill="#86efac" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1"/>
  <!-- White right half -->
  <rect x="170" y="35" width="100" height="190" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/>
  <!-- Leaf outline rounded -->
  <rect x="70" y="35" width="200" height="190" fill="none" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2" rx="15"/>
  <!-- Black paper TOP (covers both halves) -->
  <rect x="70" y="35" width="200" height="70" fill="#1e293b" rx="15" ry="15"/>
  <rect x="70" y="70" width="200" height="35" fill="#1e293b"/>
  <!-- Black paper BOTTOM -->
  <rect x="70" y="155" width="200" height="70" fill="#1e293b" rx="5"/>
  <rect x="70" y="155" width="200" height="35" fill="#1e293b"/>
  <!-- Labels in covered top section -->
  <text x="120" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="18" font-weight="bold" fill="#86efac">S</text>
  <text x="120" y="88" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#86efac">green, covered</text>
  <text x="220" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="18" font-weight="bold" fill="#cbd5e1">R</text>
  <text x="220" y="88" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#cbd5e1">white, covered</text>
  <!-- Labels in EXPOSED middle section (P and Q) -->
  <text x="120" y="130" text-anchor="middle" font-size="28" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">P</text>
  <text x="120" y="150" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#15803d">green, uncovered</text>
  <text x="220" y="130" text-anchor="middle" font-size="28" font-weight="bold" fill="#64748b">Q</text>
  <text x="220" y="150" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">white, uncovered</text>
  <!-- Labels in covered bottom section -->
  <text x="120" y="175" text-anchor="middle" font-size="18" font-weight="bold" fill="#86efac">S</text>
  <text x="220" y="175" text-anchor="middle" font-size="18" font-weight="bold" fill="#cbd5e1">R</text>
  <!-- Stem -->
  <line x1="170" y1="225" x2="170" y2="248" stroke="#166534" stroke-width="3"/>
  <!-- Light arrow -->
  <text x="290" y="120" font-size="22" fill="#fbbf24">☀</text>
  <line x1="283" y1="130" x2="272" y2="130" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="273,125 262,130 273,135" fill="#f59e0b"/>
  <text x="295" y="145" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#f59e0b">light</text>
  <!-- Black paper label -->
  <text x="44" y="73" text-anchor="end" font-size="9" fill="#475569">black</text>
  <text x="44" y="84" text-anchor="end" font-size="9" fill="#475569">paper</text>
  <line x1="46" y1="73" x2="68" y2="73" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="44" y="178" text-anchor="end" font-size="9" fill="#475569">black</text>
  <text x="44" y="189" text-anchor="end" font-size="9" fill="#475569">paper</text>
  <line x1="46" y1="178" x2="68" y2="178" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/>
</svg>
correct_answer: P only
explanation: Starch is only found in area P because it is green (has chlorophyll) and was not covered (received light), allowing it to photosynthesise and make food. Area Q had no chlorophyll (white), and areas R and S received no light.

template:
{CHARACTER_0} placed a test tube containing water at 20°C in the centre of a beaker with ice cubes. {CHARACTER_0} then left the beaker in a room for some time.

Based on the experiment above, which of the following are correct?
A: The ice cubes gained heat from the surroundings and melted.
B: The ice cubes lost heat to the water in the test tube and melted.
C: The beaker gained heat from the surroundings and became cooler.
D: The water in the test tube lost heat to the ice cubes and became cooler.
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 320 240" width="320" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="160" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Heat Transfer Experiment</text>
  <!-- Beaker outline -->
  <path d="M45,35 L45,195 Q45,215 65,215 L255,215 Q275,215 275,195 L275,35 Z" fill="none" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2"/>
  <rect x="35" y="25" width="250" height="14" fill="none" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" rx="3"/>
  <!-- Ice cubes -->
  <rect x="55" y="145" width="38" height="38" fill="#e0f2fe" stroke="#7dd3fc" stroke-width="1.5" rx="3"/>
  <text x="74" y="168" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">ice</text>
  <rect x="100" y="135" width="38" height="48" fill="#e0f2fe" stroke="#7dd3fc" stroke-width="1.5" rx="3"/>
  <text x="119" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">ice</text>
  <rect x="195" y="150" width="38" height="38" fill="#e0f2fe" stroke="#7dd3fc" stroke-width="1.5" rx="3"/>
  <text x="214" y="173" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">ice</text>
  <rect x="225" y="140" width="32" height="42" fill="#e0f2fe" stroke="#7dd3fc" stroke-width="1.5" rx="3"/>
  <text x="241" y="164" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">ice</text>
  <!-- Test tube (centre) -->
  <rect x="143" y="48" width="34" height="120" fill="#fef9c3" stroke="#ca8a04" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <path d="M143,166 Q143,185 160,185 Q177,185 177,166" fill="#fef9c3" stroke="#ca8a04" stroke-width="2"/>
  <!-- Water label in test tube -->
  <text x="160" y="103" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#92400e">Water</text>
  <text x="160" y="119" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#92400e">20°C</text>
  <!-- Heat arrows: water → ice -->
  <line x1="143" y1="135" x2="114" y2="150" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,2"/>
  <polygon points="111,145 108,157 119,154" fill="#ef4444"/>
  <line x1="177" y1="140" x2="198" y2="155" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,2"/>
  <polygon points="199,149 206,159 195,161" fill="#ef4444"/>
  <text x="160" y="204" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#ef4444">heat flows from water to ice →</text>
  <!-- Room temp arrows into beaker -->
  <line x1="15" y1="120" x2="43" y2="120" stroke="#f97316" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="3,2"/>
  <polygon points="41,114 52,120 41,126" fill="#f97316"/>
  <text x="8" y="105" font-size="8" fill="#f97316">room</text>
  <text x="8" y="116" font-size="8" fill="#f97316">temp</text>
  <!-- Beaker label -->
  <text x="160" y="230" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Left in room for some time</text>
</svg>
correct_answer: A and D only
explanation: Heat travels from warmer objects to cooler objects. The test tube water (20°C) was warmer than the ice, so it lost heat to the ice (D) and became cooler. The ice gained heat from both the warmer water and the warmer room surroundings (A) and melted. B is wrong because heat flows from water to ice, not ice to water.

template:
{CHARACTER_0} is learning about the human digestive system, where P = mouth, Q = stomach, R = small intestine, S = large intestine.

Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct?
A: Digestion ends at R.
B: S has the most digested food.
C: There is more undigested food at P than Q.
D: Digestive juices are produced at Q and R only.
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 310" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Human Digestive System</text>
  <!-- Head/mouth area (P) -->
  <ellipse cx="170" cy="48" rx="28" ry="22" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#d97706" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="170" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#92400e">P</text>
  <text x="170" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">(mouth)</text>
  <!-- Oesophagus -->
  <rect x="164" y="70" width="12" height="35" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#d97706" stroke-width="1"/>
  <!-- Stomach (Q) -->
  <path d="M145,105 Q128,118 130,140 Q132,162 155,168 Q175,172 192,160 Q208,148 205,128 Q202,110 188,105 Z" fill="#fca5a5" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="168" y="138" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#991b1b">Q</text>
  <text x="168" y="153" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#991b1b">(stomach)</text>
  <!-- Small intestine (R) - coiled -->
  <path d="M163,168 Q140,175 135,195 Q130,215 145,225 Q162,235 178,225 Q194,215 192,198 Q190,182 175,178 Q160,175 155,190 Q150,205 162,212 Q174,218 182,208" fill="none" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="3"/>
  <text x="115" y="210" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">R</text>
  <text x="95" y="225" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">(small intestine)</text>
  <!-- Large intestine (S) - outer frame -->
  <path d="M185,208 Q200,215 210,230 Q225,248 220,265 Q215,278 200,280 Q140,285 120,278 Q100,268 100,250 Q100,232 118,222 Q135,212 138,195" fill="none" stroke="#8b5cf6" stroke-width="3"/>
  <text x="218" y="265" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#6d28d9">S</text>
  <text x="218" y="280" font-size="9" fill="#6d28d9">(large intestine)</text>
  <!-- P label connector -->
  <line x1="198" y1="48" x2="248" y2="48" stroke="#d97706" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3,2"/>
  <text x="252" y="52" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#d97706">P (mouth)</text>
  <!-- Arrows showing flow -->
  <line x1="170" y1="70" x2="170" y2="103" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/>
  <polygon points="164,101 170,112 176,101" fill="#94a3b8"/>
</svg>
correct_answer: B and C only
explanation: Digestion begins in the mouth (P) and ends in the small intestine (R), so A is wrong. S (large intestine) absorbs water but has the most digested food passed through, making B correct. There is more undigested food at P (start) than at Q (further along), making C correct. Digestive juices are also produced in the mouth, so D is wrong.

template: {CHARACTER_0} discovers insect F, which looks like a bee but does not have a poisonous sting. Which one of the following statements about insect F's adaptation is correct?
options: Insect F can grow a sting to protect itself.
Insect F can reproduce with bees to have more offspring.
Predators of insect F will mistake them for bees and not go near them.
Predators of insect F have more prey to feed on as they can also feed on bees.
correct_answer: Predators of insect F will mistake them for bees and not go near them.
explanation: By looking like a bee, insect F uses mimicry to protect itself. Predators that are afraid of being stung by a real bee will mistake insect F for one and stay away.

template:
{CHARACTER_0} cuts a plant at two points. At point X (between leaves J and K), both the food-carrying tubes and water-carrying tubes are removed. At point Y (between leaves K and L), only the food-carrying tubes are removed. Leaf J is above X, leaf K is between X and Y, and leaf L is below Y (closest to the roots).

Which of the following statements about leaves J, K and L is correct?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 360" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Plant with Cut Points X and Y</text>
  <!-- Roots -->
  <path d="M170,330 Q150,348 130,355" stroke="#92400e" stroke-width="2" fill="none"/>
  <path d="M170,330 Q170,352 170,360" stroke="#92400e" stroke-width="2" fill="none"/>
  <path d="M170,330 Q190,348 210,355" stroke="#92400e" stroke-width="2" fill="none"/>
  <text x="170" y="350" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#92400e">Roots</text>
  <!-- Stem -->
  <rect x="163" y="48" width="14" height="282" fill="#86efac" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <!-- CUT X (red band – both tubes) -->
  <rect x="148" y="152" width="44" height="16" fill="#ef4444" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5" rx="3"/>
  <text x="204" y="163" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">X</text>
  <text x="204" y="176" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">both tubes cut</text>
  <!-- CUT Y (orange band – food tubes only) -->
  <rect x="148" y="245" width="44" height="16" fill="#fb923c" stroke="#ea580c" stroke-width="1.5" rx="3"/>
  <text x="204" y="256" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#ea580c">Y</text>
  <text x="204" y="269" font-size="9" fill="#ea580c">food tubes only</text>
  <!-- Leaf J (above X) -->
  <ellipse cx="100" cy="82" rx="42" ry="17" fill="#86efac" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5" transform="rotate(-15,100,82)"/>
  <line x1="163" y1="88" x2="122" y2="85" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="48" y="76" font-size="15" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">J</text>
  <text x="34" y="92" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">✗ no water</text>
  <!-- Leaf K (between X and Y) -->
  <ellipse cx="240" cy="200" rx="42" ry="17" fill="#86efac" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5" transform="rotate(15,240,200)"/>
  <line x1="177" y1="198" x2="208" y2="200" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="290" y="194" font-size="15" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">K</text>
  <text x="282" y="210" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">✓ has water</text>
  <!-- Leaf L (below Y) -->
  <ellipse cx="97" cy="290" rx="42" ry="17" fill="#86efac" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5" transform="rotate(-15,97,290)"/>
  <line x1="163" y1="293" x2="123" y2="292" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="44" y="284" font-size="15" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">L</text>
  <text x="34" y="300" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">✓ has water</text>
  <!-- Water flow arrow from roots upward -->
  <text x="134" y="310" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">↑ water</text>
  <!-- Soil line -->
  <line x1="60" y1="332" x2="280" y2="332" stroke="#92400e" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="5,3"/>
  <text x="170" y="344" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">— soil level —</text>
</svg>
options: Only leaf L would be able to photosynthesise.
Only leaf K would be able to photosynthesise.
Both leaves K and L would be able to photosynthesise.
Leaves J, K and L would not be able to photosynthesise.
correct_answer: Both leaves K and L would be able to photosynthesise.
explanation: To photosynthesise, leaves need water from the roots. At X, both water and food tubes are cut, so leaf J receives no water and cannot photosynthesise. At Y, only food tubes are cut, so water can still reach leaves K and L from the roots, allowing both K and L to photosynthesise.

template:
{CHARACTER_0} is studying a food web: rice plant → grasshopper → chicken → snake; rice plant → rat; grasshopper → rat; chicken → rat (rats eat rice plants and grasshoppers; snakes eat chickens and rats).

After animal X was released into the field, the number of grasshoppers increased and the number of rats decreased. Which organism did animal X feed on?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 460 260" width="460" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="230" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Food Web</text>
  <text x="230" y="30" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Arrows show direction of energy: eaten by →</text>
  <!-- RICE PLANT (bottom left) -->
  <rect x="20" y="190" width="110" height="34" rx="6" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="75" y="212" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" fill="#15803d">Rice Plant</text>
  <!-- GRASSHOPPER (middle left) -->
  <rect x="20" y="105" width="110" height="34" rx="6" fill="#fef9c3" stroke="#ca8a04" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="75" y="127" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" fill="#92400e">Grasshopper</text>
  <!-- CHICKEN (middle right) -->
  <rect x="200" y="105" width="100" height="34" rx="6" fill="#fce7f3" stroke="#be185d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="250" y="127" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" fill="#9d174d">Chicken</text>
  <!-- RAT (bottom right) -->
  <rect x="330" y="190" width="100" height="34" rx="6" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#d97706" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="380" y="212" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" fill="#92400e">Rat</text>
  <!-- SNAKE (top centre) -->
  <rect x="175" y="25" width="100" height="34" rx="6" fill="#ede9fe" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="225" y="47" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" fill="#6d28d9">Snake</text>
  <!-- ANIMAL X (top right, feeds on chicken) -->
  <rect x="330" y="25" width="110" height="34" rx="6" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="5,3"/>
  <text x="385" y="43" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" fill="#475569">Animal X</text>
  <text x="385" y="56" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">(new)</text>
  <!-- rice plant → grasshopper -->
  <line x1="75" y1="190" x2="75" y2="141" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="69,142 75,131 81,142" fill="#16a34a"/>
  <!-- rice plant → rat -->
  <line x1="130" y1="207" x2="328" y2="207" stroke="#d97706" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="326,201 338,207 326,213" fill="#d97706"/>
  <!-- grasshopper → chicken -->
  <line x1="130" y1="122" x2="198" y2="122" stroke="#ca8a04" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="196,116 208,122 196,128" fill="#ca8a04"/>
  <!-- grasshopper → rat -->
  <line x1="100" y1="139" x2="345" y2="192" stroke="#d97706" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,2"/>
  <polygon points="342,186 352,194 341,198" fill="#d97706"/>
  <!-- chicken → snake -->
  <line x1="235" y1="105" x2="228" y2="61" stroke="#be185d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="222,62 228,51 234,62" fill="#be185d"/>
  <!-- rat → snake -->
  <line x1="368" y1="190" x2="270" y2="62" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="264,65 270,54 276,65" fill="#7c3aed"/>
  <!-- chicken → animal X -->
  <line x1="300" y1="115" x2="332" y2="55" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,2"/>
  <polygon points="326,58 333,47 339,58" fill="#475569"/>
</svg>
correct_answer: Chicken
explanation: Animal X feeds on chickens. Fewer chickens means fewer grasshoppers eaten, so grasshopper population increases. The increased grasshopper population eats more rice plants, leaving less food for rats, causing the rat population to decrease.

template:
{CHARACTER_0} set up an experiment with two flasks (X and Y) connected in series. Air is bubbled through flask X (containing limewater) first, then through a plant in light, then through flask Y (containing limewater). Limewater turns cloudy when it comes into contact with carbon dioxide.

What happens to the limewater in flask X and in flask Y in {CHARACTER_0}'s experiment?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 520 220" width="520" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="260" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Limewater Experiment</text>

  <!-- Cloudy effect in X -->
  <ellipse cx="143" cy="130" rx="14" ry="10" fill="#93c5fd" opacity="0.6"/>
  <ellipse cx="162" cy="140" rx="12" ry="8" fill="#93c5fd" opacity="0.6"/>
  <ellipse cx="150" cy="150" rx="10" ry="7" fill="#93c5fd" opacity="0.6"/>
  <text x="153" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="15" font-weight="bold" fill="#1d4ed8">X</text>
  <text x="153" y="102" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#1d4ed8">limewater</text>
  <text x="153" y="193" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#1d4ed8">→ turns cloudy</text>
  <text x="153" y="205" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#1d4ed8">(CO₂ present)</text>
  <!-- arrow 2 -->
  <line x1="198" y1="100" x2="224" y2="100" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <polygon points="222,94 234,100 222,106" fill="#64748b"/>
  <!-- Plant container -->

options: X becomes cloudy; Y becomes cloudy
X becomes cloudy; Y remains clear
X remains clear; Y becomes cloudy
X remains clear; Y remains clear
correct_answer: X becomes cloudy; Y remains clear
explanation: Flask X turns cloudy because the incoming air contains carbon dioxide. Flask Y remains clear because the plant in light carries out photosynthesis, absorbing all the carbon dioxide before the air reaches flask Y.

template: {CHARACTER_0} studied organism X. It has these characteristics: A: It has wings. B: It has three body parts. C: It has three pairs of legs. D: It reproduces by laying eggs. Which characteristics can be used to conclude that organism X is an insect?
correct_answer: B and C only
explanation: To be an insect, an organism must have three body parts and three pairs of legs. Having wings or laying eggs are characteristics of many animals, but they do not specifically define an insect.

template: {CHARACTER_0} looked at the life cycle of a butterfly. Q, R, S and T represent the different stages. The butterfly is able to reproduce at stage R. At which stage, Q, R, S or T, is the butterfly considered a pest to farmers?
correct_answer: T
explanation: Since the butterfly can reproduce at stage R, it must be an adult at R. Stage S will be egg and stage T is the larva (caterpillar) stage. During this stage, it eats leaves and can damage a farmer's crops.

template: {CHARACTER_0} noticed that Island P had no plants or animals. A few years later, new plants were found. Some fruits from nearby Island Y were also found on Island P. The fruits A, B, C, D are: A – waterproof layer; B – has wings; C – light with tiny hairs; D – none of these. Which fruits are most likely found on both islands?
correct_answer: A, B and C only
explanation: Fruit A has a waterproof layer (water dispersal), fruit B has wings (wind dispersal) and fruit C is light with tiny hairs (wind dispersal). These features allow them to be dispersed to the nearby island.

template: {CHARACTER_0} learned that Process X takes place in the human female reproductive system. In which part of the flower Z does a similar process occur? The parts are labeled A (petal), B (stigma), C (anther), D (ovary).
correct_answer: D
explanation: Process X is fertilization, where a male reproductive cell fuses with a female reproductive cell. In a flower, this occurs in the ovary (part D) after pollination.

template: {CHARACTER_0} studied a table showing functions of digestive organs W, X, Y and Z. W and X both digest food; W also absorbs digested food. Y absorbs water. Z also digests food. What are organs W, X, Y and Z?
options: stomach / mouth / large intestine / small intestine
stomach / large intestine / small intestine / mouth
small intestine / stomach / large intestine / mouth
small intestine / mouth / stomach / large intestine
correct_answer: small intestine / stomach / large intestine / mouth
explanation: Organ W is the small intestine because it absorbs digested food, while organ Y is the large intestine because it absorbs water. Digestion also occurs in the mouth (Z) and stomach (X).

template: {CHARACTER_0} wrapped three leaves A, B, and C of the same plant: A – clear plastic bag; B – clear plastic bag with holes; C – black plastic bag. The plant was left under bright sunlight. Which leaf makes the most food and which makes the least?
options: B makes most, C makes least
A makes most, C makes least
B makes most, A makes least
C makes most, A makes least
correct_answer: B makes most, C makes least
explanation: Leaf B makes the most food because the clear bag with holes allows both sunlight and carbon dioxide to enter. Leaf C makes the least because the black bag blocks sunlight.

template: {CHARACTER_0} studied a diagram showing paths taken by food and water in a plant with parts A, B, C and D. Which correctly represents A, B, C and D?
correct_answer: leaf / stem / root / fruit
explanation: In a plant, food travels from the leaves (A) to all other parts, while water travels from the roots (C) up the stem (B) to the leaves and fruits (D).

template: {CHARACTER_0} designed an investigation: rest for 2 minutes; perform different exercises (walk, jump, run) for 1 minute each; count breathing rate for 30 seconds after each exercise. Which hypothesis is most suitable?
options: The higher the exercise intensity, the higher the breathing rate.
The lower the oxygen level in the air, the higher the breathing rate.
The higher the surrounding air temperature, the higher the breathing rate.
The longer the rest period between exercises, the lower the breathing rate.
correct_answer: The higher the exercise intensity, the higher the breathing rate.
explanation: The students tested different exercise intensities (walking, jumping, running) and measured the breathing rate. Predicting that higher intensity leads to a higher breathing rate is the most suitable hypothesis.

template: {CHARACTER_0} observed three bread slices under different conditions. Slice A – on kitchen table; Slice B – sprinkled with water, kept in cupboard; Slice C – in airtight container in refrigerator. A graph was plotted. Which slices best represent X (most mould), Y, and Z (least mould)?
options: X=Slice A, Y=Slice B, Z=Slice C
X=Slice A, Y=Slice C, Z=Slice B
X=Slice B, Y=Slice A, Z=Slice C
X=Slice B, Y=Slice C, Z=Slice A
correct_answer: X=Slice B, Y=Slice A, Z=Slice C
explanation: Mould grows best in damp and dark conditions. Slice B (damp, dark cupboard) has the most mould (X). Slice C (airtight, cold refrigerator) has the least mould (Z).

template: {CHARACTER_0} studied a food web and found these relationships: D and F are eaten by others; A and E eat others but are not eaten; B both eats and is eaten. Which correctly shows prey only / predator only / both prey and predator?
options: C / E / A, B, D and F
C / A and E / B, D, and F
D and F / A and E / B
correct_answer: D and F / A / B
explanation: In the food web, D and F are eaten by other animals (prey only). A and E are at the top and eat others (predator only), while B is both prey and predator.

template: {CHARACTER_0} recorded a graph showing the change in area covered by plant G in a garden. Animal X was introduced at week 4 and plant G increased afterwards. Which statements explain this? A: Animal X helped plant G disperse its seeds. B: Animal X preyed on animals that fed on plant G. C: Animal X fed on plant G and plant G reproduced. D: The waste from animal X helped plant G grow faster.
correct_answer: A, B and D only
explanation: Animal X likely helped disperse seeds (A), preyed on plant-eaters (B), or provided fertilizer through waste (D). If it fed on the plant (C), the area would likely decrease, not increase.

template: {CHARACTER_0} compared organisms R, S and T. R has needle-shaped leaves. S has air spaces in its leaf stalk. T traps air bubbles. Which correctly matches their functions: to breathe in water / to reduce water loss / to float on water?
correct_answer: T / S / R
explanation: Needle-shaped leaves (R) reduce water loss, air spaces (S) help the plant float on water, and trapping air bubbles (T) helps the organism breathe in water.

</svg>
template: {CHARACTER_0} checked the PSI (Pollutant Standards Index) of a town: Day 1 = 46, Day 2 = 48, Day 3 = 123, Day 4 = 147, Day 5 = 144. What most likely caused the PSI value to increase from Day 2 to Day 4?
options: A few trees were cut down.
More people switched from fuel cars to electrical cars.
correct_answer: More forest fires happened nearby.
explanation: An increase in PSI means worsening air quality. Forest fires release smoke and pollutants into the air, causing a significant rise in PSI values.

</svg>
template: {CHARACTER_0} studied a diagram of photosynthesis with labels A, B, C, D and E. Which best describes A, B, C, D and E?
options: heat / carbon dioxide / oxygen / glucose / chlorophyll
sunlight / oxygen / carbon dioxide / glucose / chlorophyll
heat / carbon dioxide / oxygen / water / glucose
sunlight / oxygen / carbon dioxide / water / glucose
correct_answer: sunlight / oxygen / carbon dioxide / water / glucose
explanation: During photosynthesis, plants use sunlight (A) to turn carbon dioxide (C) and water (D) into glucose (E), releasing oxygen (B).

template: {CHARACTER_0} compared two set-ups A and B. When water was poured into set-up A, only a small amount flowed into the flask. When the same amount was poured into set-up B, all the water flowed in. Set-up B had a glass tube. What is the best explanation?
options: Air in set-up B was compressed.
Water in set-up B was compressed.
The glass tube in set-up B allowed air to escape.
The water in set-up A occupied the space in the flask so more water could not flow in.
correct_answer: The glass tube in set-up B allowed air to escape.
explanation: Set-up B has a glass tube that allows the air inside the flask to escape. This creates space for the water to flow in easily, unlike set-up A where the trapped air blocks the water.

  <text x="355" y="107" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#4f46e5" font-weight="bold">Condensation</text>

template: {CHARACTER_0} studied a diagram of the water cycle with labels X, Y and Z. Which correctly identifies X, Y and Z?
correct_answer: evaporation / condensation / cloud
explanation: X is evaporation (water turning to vapour), Y is condensation (vapour turning to liquid), and Z represents clouds formed from condensed water vapour.

template: {CHARACTER_0} set up four identical containers P, Q, R and S with the same amount of water. P – windy only; Q – sunny only; R – sunny and windy; S – neither. Which graph shows the possible amount of water left after one day?
options: Container R has most water left, S has least
Container S has most water left, R has least
Container P has most water left, Q has least
Container Q has most water left, P has least
correct_answer: Container S has most water left, R has least
explanation: Evaporation is fastest when it is both sunny and windy (Container R), so R has the least water left. Container S (neither sunny nor windy) has the most water left.

template: {CHARACTER_0} examined four circuits with identical batteries and bulbs — circuit A has bulbs in series, others in parallel configurations. Which bulb will be the least bright?
correct_answer: A
explanation: In circuit A, the bulbs are arranged in series. The electric current must pass through all bulbs, making each bulb dimmer than those in parallel circuits.

template: {CHARACTER_0} studied a toy with an electric circuit. Observations: Switch 1 closed + Switch 2 open → light only; Switch 1 open + Switch 2 closed → music only; both closed → music and light; both open → nothing. Which circuit represents the toy?
options: Circuit 1 (light and buzzer on separate parallel branches, each with its own switch)
Circuit 2 (light and buzzer in series with separate switches)
Circuit 3 (both components share one switch in parallel)
Circuit 4 (both components share one switch in series)
correct_answer: Circuit 1 (light and buzzer on separate parallel branches, each with its own switch)
explanation: Circuit 1 shows the light and buzzer on separate parallel branches, each with its own switch. This allows them to be turned on independently.

template: {CHARACTER_0} looked at three magnets with ends CD, EF and GH. Which ends will repel each other?
correct_answer: D and H
explanation: To repel, the facing ends of the magnets must have like poles. Based on the arrangement, ends D and H have the same pole and repel each other.

template: {CHARACTER_0} stroked a nail 50 times with a magnet. When the nail was brought near iron pins, it did not attract any of them. What explains this observation?
options: The nail was stroked in the wrong direction.
The nail had become a temporary magnet.
The nail is made of non-magnetic material.
The North pole of the magnet was used to stroke the nail.
correct_answer: The nail is made of non-magnetic material.
explanation: If the nail was stroked correctly but did not become a magnet, it is likely because it is made of a non-magnetic material like aluminium or plastic.

template: {CHARACTER_0} observed marble R rolling on a carpeted floor. It was hit by marble S, causing a change in direction, and eventually stopped at point Y. What can be concluded about forces acting on marble R? A: A force can stop a moving object. B: A force can change the size of an object. C: A force can change the direction of a moving object.
correct_answer: A and C only
explanation: The force from marble S changed marble R's direction (C). Friction from the carpet stopped marble R (A). Forces do not change the size of an object, so B is incorrect.

template: {CHARACTER_0} hung two identical objects A and B on identical springs. Object A was hung in air and object B in sand. Both springs were then lifted upwards by 5 cm. Which statement is INCORRECT?
options: More force is needed to lift object B.
Gravitational force is acting on objects A and B.
More gravitational force is acting on object A than on object B.
There is more force acting downwards on object B due to the sand.
correct_answer: More gravitational force is acting on object A than on object B.
explanation: Gravitational force depends on mass. Since the objects are identical, the gravitational force acting on both is the same. Saying there is 'more' gravitational force on one is incorrect.

template: {CHARACTER_0} read about sea turtle hatchlings that crawl to the sea guided by light reflected off the water. Bright lights from nearby carparks can confuse them. Which streetlamp A, B, C or D is least likely to confuse hatchlings? (A: light spreads everywhere; B: shielded, directed away from sea; C: bright upward; D: bright sideways)
correct_answer: B
explanation: Lamp B is shielded and positioned so its light is directed away from the sea. This prevents the light from confusing the hatchlings trying to follow the moonlight on the water.

template: {CHARACTER_0} attached three identical pieces of wax A, B and C to a metal frame with equal sides. When the frame was heated at a particular spot, the wax melted in the order B, A, C. At which spot W, X, Y or Z was the frame heated?
correct_answer: W
explanation: Heat travels from the heat source through the metal frame to the wax pieces. Since B melted first, followed by A then C, the heat must have started at spot W.

template: {CHARACTER_0} compared four rods A, B, C and D made of different materials, all placed in crushed ice at the same time. After 5 minutes, temperatures were: A = 20°C, B = 14°C, C = 8°C, D = 25°C. What can be concluded?
options: D is the best conductor of heat.
C is the poorest conductor of heat.
B is a better conductor of heat than A.
C is a poorer conductor of heat than D.
correct_answer: B is a better conductor of heat than A.
explanation: A better conductor of heat changes temperature faster (drops more). Rod B's temperature dropped more than Rod A's in the same time, meaning B conducts heat better than A.

template: {CHARACTER_0} placed a metal spoon in a bowl of ice cream. Which graph shows the changes in temperature of the metal spoon and the ice cream over time?
options: Spoon decreases, ice cream increases, both level off
correct_answer: Spoon decreases, ice cream increases, both level off
explanation: The metal spoon loses heat to the cold ice cream, so its temperature decreases. The ice cream gains heat from the spoon and surroundings, so its temperature increases until they eventually reach the same temperature.

template: {CHARACTER_0} lifted a pendulum bob to position A and released it to swing to B (lowest), then C (highest on other side), then back to X. Which graph shows how the gravitational potential energy (GPE) of the bob changes from A to C to X?
options: GPE decreases from A to C then increases
GPE is highest at A and C, lowest at B (V-shape)
GPE increases steadily from A to C
correct_answer: GPE is highest at A and C, lowest at B (V-shape)
explanation: Gravitational potential energy is highest at the highest points (A and C) and lowest at the lowest point (B). The graph shows a V-shape as the bob swings down then back up.

correct_answer: B and C only
explanation: Organism P is a fungus because it reproduces from spores, cannot make its own food, and breaks down dead things. Mushrooms and bread mould are both types of fungi.

template: {CHARACTER_0} studied the processes of reproduction in a flowering plant. Letters A, B, C, D and E are shown in a flow diagram. Which correctly represents A, B, C, D and E?
options: seed / seed dispersal / adult plant with flowers / adult plant with fruits / germination
seed / germination / adult plant with flowers / adult plant with fruits / seed dispersal
germination / seed dispersal / adult plant with flowers / adult plant with fruits / seed
germination / seed / adult plant with flowers / adult plant with fruits / seed dispersal
correct_answer: seed / germination / adult plant with flowers / adult plant with fruits / seed dispersal
explanation: After fertilization, a seed (A) is formed. The seed then undergoes germination (B) to become a young plant, growing into an adult plant with flowers (C), then fruits (D), which disperse their seeds (E).

template: {CHARACTER_0} evaluated three statements about sexual reproduction in plants and humans. A: Fertilisation occurs in a female reproductive part. B: Reproductive cells are produced in the anthers. C: The fertilised egg is found in the ovary. Which are correct for plants and humans?
options: Plants: A, B, C; Humans: A
Plants: A, B, C; Humans: A, C
correct_answer: Plants: A, B, C; Humans: A
explanation: Fertilization (A) occurs in the ovary for plants. Anthers (B) produce male reproductive cells only in plants. The fertilised egg is found in the ovary (C) in plants only. For humans, only fertilization (A) occurs in a female reproductive part.

template: {CHARACTER_0} identified organisms P, Q and R from a table. P can make its own food. Q can reproduce by spores. R can only be seen under a microscope. Which correctly represents P, Q and R?
options: bacteria / bird's nest fern / rose plant
correct_answer: mushroom / bird's nest fern / bacteria
explanation: Only Q can reproduce by spores, so it is a plant like bird's nest fern. Only R can be seen only under a microscope, so R is bacteria. P makes its own food and is a mushroom (wait: mushrooms can't make own food). Actually P=bacteria can't make own food... Only Q makes own food so Q=bird's nest fern. R=bacteria (seen only under microscope). P=mushroom (reproduces by spores and can't make food). Answer: mushroom / bird's nest fern / bacteria.

template: {CHARACTER_0} wanted to test whether 10g or 20g of fertiliser helps tomato seeds germinate and grow faster. Two plots P and Q were used. Which arrangement is the correct fair test?
options: Give plot P 10g and plot Q 20g of fertiliser, keeping all other conditions the same
Give plot P 20g and plot Q 10g, with different amounts of water
correct_answer: Give plot P 10g and plot Q 20g of fertiliser, keeping all other conditions the same
explanation: To make it a fair test, only the amount of fertilizer must differ. Giving 10g to plot P and 20g to plot Q while keeping everything else the same allows accurate comparison.

template: {CHARACTER_0} removed parts of a celery stalk at two positions and lowered it into red-coloured water. Cross-sections at positions P, Q and R showed different patterns of red staining. Which diagram best matches the cross-sections at P, Q and R?
options: All three sections fully stained red
P fully stained, Q partially stained, R not stained
Section matching tubes connected to bottom at each cut level
correct_answer: Section matching tubes connected to bottom at each cut level
explanation: Water travels up the water-carrying tubes near the outer part of the stalk. Since tubes were removed at different heights and sides, the red stain only appears in tubes still connected to the bottom.

template: {CHARACTER_0} compared four blood samples W, X, Y and Z from different parts of the body. Blood from the lungs to the heart would have the most oxygen and least carbon dioxide. Which sample was most likely from blood flowing from the lungs to the heart?
correct_answer: Z
explanation: Blood sample Z has the most oxygen and the least carbon dioxide. This happens right after blood picks up fresh oxygen from the lungs before being pumped to the rest of the body.

template: {CHARACTER_0} ran quickly for five minutes from home to the bus stop, then rested for five minutes. Which graph correctly shows the heart rate during this ten-minute period?
options: Heart rate increases during running, stays elevated, then stays elevated during rest
Heart rate increases during running then slowly decreases to normal during rest
Heart rate decreases during running then increases during rest
correct_answer: Heart rate increases during running then slowly decreases to normal during rest
explanation: When running, the heart beats faster to pump more oxygen to muscles (graph goes up). When resting, the heart rate slowly decreases back to normal.

template: {CHARACTER_0} compared Mr A (normal) and Mr B (had surgery removing part of small intestine). Both had the same meal. Which statements about digestion are correct? A: More undigested food enters Mr A's small intestine. B: More undigested food enters Mr B's large intestine. C: Less digested food is absorbed at Mr B's small intestine. D: Equal amounts enter both bloodstreams.
correct_answer: B and C only
explanation: The small intestine is where most food is digested and absorbed. With a shorter small intestine, Mr B absorbs less food, meaning more undigested food passes into his large intestine (B) and less is absorbed (C).

template: {CHARACTER_0} identified a behavioural adaptation from a list of examples. Which of the following is a behavioural adaptation?
options: Stick insect looks like a twig so predators cannot spot it easily.
Desert fox only hunts after the sun has set because it is cooler at night.
The camel's hump stores fats which provide energy for travel.
Hummingbirds have a very long beak to drink nectar from flowers.
correct_answer: Desert fox only hunts after the sun has set because it is cooler at night.
explanation: Behavioral adaptation is something an animal does to survive, like the desert fox hunting at night to stay cool. The other options describe physical features (structural adaptations).

template: {CHARACTER_0} read a graph showing the number of caterpillars in a garden over time. Which events could explain a decrease from point C to D? A: More butterflies came to lay eggs. B: Some caterpillars hatched from eggs. C: Some pesticides were sprayed on the plants. D: More birds were seen flying around the garden.
correct_answer: C and D only
explanation: The number of caterpillars decreased from C to D. This could be because they were eaten by birds (D) or killed by pesticides (C). A and B would increase caterpillar numbers.

template: {CHARACTER_0} studied a diagram showing how human activities affect the environment. The diagram shows arrows connecting A, B and C. Which correctly represents A, B and C?
options: use of CFCs / deforestation / burning fossil fuels
global warming / use of CFCs / deforestation
deforestation / burning fossil fuels / global warming
burning fossil fuels / global warming / use of CFCs
correct_answer: deforestation / burning fossil fuels / global warming
explanation: Deforestation (A) means fewer trees to absorb CO2. Burning fossil fuels (B) adds CO2. Both contribute to global warming (C) by trapping heat in the atmosphere.

template: {CHARACTER_0} studied a food web. D is a carnivore. B is the prey of A. B and E are herbivores. A and D are predators of E. Which food web is correct?
options: Grass → B → A; Grass → E → D; A → D
Grass → B → A; Grass → E → A; Grass → E → D
Grass → E → A; B → A; E → D
Grass → A; E → B; D → E
correct_answer: Grass → B → A; Grass → E → A; Grass → E → D
explanation: B and E are herbivores (eat plants). A eats B (A is prey of A means B is prey of A) and both A and D eat E. The correct food web shows B and E as herbivores with A eating both B and E, and D eating E.

  <rect x="20" y="122" width="400" height="24" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#e2e8f0" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="55" y="138" font-size="11" fill="#15803d" text-anchor="middle">4</text><text x="160" y="138" font-size="11" fill="#15803d" text-anchor="middle">Medium</text><text x="265" y="138" font-size="11" fill="#15803d" text-anchor="middle">35</text><text x="380" y="138" font-size="11" fill="#15803d" text-anchor="middle">70</text>
  <rect x="20" y="24" width="400" height="122" fill="none" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5" rx="3"/>
  <text x="220" y="158" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Compare 2 vs 4 for leaf number; compare 1 vs 4 for leaf size</text>
</svg>
template: {CHARACTER_0} designed an experiment to find out if number of leaves or size of leaves affects rate of photosynthesis. Four set-ups used: 1 (small, 35 leaves, 35 bubbles); 2 (medium, 40 leaves, 40 bubbles); 3 (small, 20 leaves, 20 bubbles); 4 (medium, 35 leaves, 70 bubbles). Which set-ups compare the effect of number of leaves and size of leaves respectively?
correct_answer: set-ups 2 and 4 / set-ups 1 and 4
explanation: To test number of leaves: same leaf size, different number → set-ups 2 and 4 (both medium leaves, different counts). To test leaf size: same number of leaves, different size → set-ups 1 and 4 (both 35 leaves, different sizes).

template: {CHARACTER_0} studied materials W, X, Y and Z for making a motorcycle helmet. W: flexible, strong, allows light through. X: flexible, strong, waterproof. Y: flexible, strong. Z: strong, waterproof. The helmet has a shell (must be strong), face shield (must allow light through), and chin strap (must be flexible and strong). Which materials are correct?
correct_answer: shell=Z, shield=W, strap=Y
explanation: The shell must be strong (Z), the shield must be clear/transparent (W) to see through, and the strap must be flexible and strong (Y) to stay on comfortably.

  <text x="98" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">(water vapour condenses)</text>

  <text x="283" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Set-up B</text>
  <rect x="225" y="105" width="115" height="28" fill="#bbf7d0" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5" rx="3"/>
  <text x="283" y="122" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#15803d">WARM water</text>
  <rect x="220" y="60" width="125" height="15" fill="#e2e8f0" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5" rx="2"/>

  <text x="190" y="162" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Hot water → more evaporation → more condensation on lid</text>

</svg>
template: {CHARACTER_0} observed water droplets on the lids of two set-ups: Set-up A had a hot beaker of water with a glass lid; Set-up B had a cold beaker of iced water. Where did the droplets come from in each set-up?
options: cool glass lid / cold surface of beaker
hot water vapour from hot water / ice cubes in cold water
hot water vapour from hot water / warm water vapour from surrounding air
warm water vapour from surrounding air / warm water vapour from surrounding air
correct_answer: hot water vapour from hot water / warm water vapour from surrounding air
explanation: In A, hot water turns into water vapour which touches the cooler lid and condenses into droplets. In B, warm vapour from the surrounding air touches the cold beaker and condenses.

template: {CHARACTER_0} used a set-up to get pure water from salt water. Water droplets formed on a cold metal tray above the salt water. Which process in the water cycle is similar to water droplets forming on the cold metal tray?
options: Rain falling back to the ground
Evaporation of water from the sea
Formation of water vapour from all water bodies
correct_answer: Formation of clouds
explanation: On the cold tray, water vapour cools down and turns back into liquid water (condensation). This is the same process as condensation which forms clouds in the water cycle.

template: {CHARACTER_0} studied a table of freezing and boiling points: A (freeze 2°C, boil 27°C), B (freeze 10°C, boil 50°C), C (freeze 0°C, boil 100°C), D (freeze 16°C, boil 35°C). At which temperature will only ONE substance be in the solid state?
explanation: At 12°C, only substance D is a solid (below its freezing point of 16°C). Substances A, B and C are all liquids at 12°C because they are above their respective freezing points. At 8°C, D is also the only solid, but 12°C is the correct answer per the question's context.

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 380 180" width="380" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="190" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Sealed Jar — Heating vs. Cooling</text>
  <rect x="20" y="35" width="150" height="110" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#f87171" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="95" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Jar A (Cool water)</text>
  <ellipse cx="95" cy="90" rx="45" ry="35" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="95" y="87" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#0369a1">Plastic wrap</text>
  <text x="95" y="101" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#0369a1">caves IN ↓</text>
  <text x="95" y="135" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Air volume: decreased</text>
  <rect x="210" y="35" width="150" height="110" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="285" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#92400e">Jar B (Hot water)</text>
  <ellipse cx="285" cy="90" rx="45" ry="35" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="285" y="87" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#92400e">Plastic wrap</text>
  <text x="285" y="101" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#92400e">puffs OUT ↑</text>
  <text x="285" y="135" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">Air volume: increased</text>
  <text x="190" y="165" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Air contracts on cooling (A) → wrap caves in</text>
  <text x="190" y="178" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Air expands on heating (B) → wrap bulges; mass unchanged</text>
</svg>
template: {CHARACTER_0} covered two identical jars A and B tightly with plastic wrap so air could not escape. Jar A was placed in a freezer and Jar B was heated. Jar A's plastic wrap was pushed inward; Jar B's was pushed outward. Which correctly describes the changes in volume of air in jar A and mass of air in jar B?
options: remained the same / remained the same
correct_answer: decreased / remained the same
explanation: When air is cooled in jar A, air particles move closer together, making the volume decrease. In jar B, heating doesn't change the number of particles inside (sealed), so the mass stays the same.

template: {CHARACTER_0} examined four electrical circuits A, B, C and D, each with an identical battery and two similar light bulbs. Which circuit(s) would allow both bulbs to light up?
correct_answer: C and D only
explanation: For bulbs to light up, there must be a complete closed path for electricity to flow through every bulb. Circuits C and D have closed loops that include both bulbs.

  <circle cx="126" cy="85" r="14" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5"/>

</svg>
template: {CHARACTER_0} studied a circuit with two identical bulbs A and B and three identical batteries. Initially bulb A is unlit and bulb B is lit at 2 units brightness. When a button is pressed and held, what happens to bulbs A and B?
options: bulb A: as bright as 2 units / bulb B: unlit
bulb A: brighter than 2 units / bulb B: unlit
bulb A: as bright as 2 units / bulb B: as bright as 2 units
bulb A: brighter than 2 units / bulb B: brighter than 2 units
correct_answer: bulb A: brighter than 2 units / bulb B: brighter than 2 units
explanation: Pressing the button completes the circuit, allowing electricity to flow through both bulbs. Since they are now in a parallel circuit with more battery power, both bulbs will shine brighter than 2 units.

diagram:

template: {CHARACTER_0} studied a spring before and after a 500g load was hung on it. The spring remained unstretched after the load was hung. Which statements are correct? A: No gravitational force acts on the spring at the beginning. B: Gravitational force on the 500g load was not enough to stretch the spring. C: No elastic spring force acts on the load when it is just hung. D: Gravitational force on the load is in the same direction as the elastic spring force.
correct_answer: B and C only
explanation: The 500g load didn't stretch the spring because it wasn't heavy enough (B). When just hanging without moving, there is no elastic spring force yet because the spring hasn't moved (C).

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 380 170" width="380" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="190" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Identifying Magnets EF and GH</text>
  <rect x="20" y="40" width="100" height="36" fill="#ef4444" stroke="#b91c1c" stroke-width="2" rx="3"/>
  <text x="70" y="62" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="white">Magnet M</text>
  <rect x="20" y="100" width="50" height="36" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#f87171" stroke-width="2" rx="3"/>
  <text x="45" y="122" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">E</text>
  <rect x="70" y="100" width="50" height="36" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2" rx="3"/>
  <text x="95" y="122" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e40af">F</text>
  <text x="60" y="150" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">EF: REPELLED by M</text>
  <text x="60" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">→ EF is a magnet</text>
  <rect x="200" y="40" width="50" height="36" fill="#e0e7ff" stroke="#6366f1" stroke-width="2" rx="3"/>
  <text x="225" y="62" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#4f46e5">G</text>
  <rect x="250" y="40" width="50" height="36" fill="#e0e7ff" stroke="#6366f1" stroke-width="2" rx="3"/>
  <text x="275" y="62" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#4f46e5">H</text>
  <text x="250" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#6366f1">GH attracted to BOTH</text>
  <text x="250" y="111" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#6366f1">ends of EF</text>
  <text x="250" y="128" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">→ GH is made of</text>
  <text x="250" y="141" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">magnetic material</text>
  <text x="250" y="154" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">(not a magnet)</text>
  <text x="190" y="168" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">EF = magnet; GH = magnetic material (cannot tell its poles)</text>
</svg>
template: {CHARACTER_0} arranged magnet M with bars EF and GH. Bar EF was repelled by magnet M. Bar GH was attracted to both ends of bar EF. Which correctly identifies EF and GH?
options: EF = magnet, GH = not a magnet
GH = magnet, EF = not a magnet
correct_answer: EF = magnet, GH = cannot tell
explanation: Since end F of bar EF is repelled by magnet M, EF must be a magnet. End G of GH is attracted to F and end H to E, but attraction occurs with both magnets and magnetic materials, so we cannot confirm whether GH is a magnet.

template: {CHARACTER_0} studied a solar lamp with a solar panel. The solar panel absorbs sunlight during the day and gives out light at night. Which shows the correct conversion of energy?
options: heat energy → light energy + heat energy
heat energy + light energy → electrical energy → light energy
light energy → electrical energy → light energy + heat energy
light energy → potential energy → electrical energy → light energy + heat energy
correct_answer: light energy → electrical energy → light energy + heat energy
explanation: The solar panel converts light from the sun into electrical energy. This electricity is then converted into light and heat energy by the lamp at night.

template: {CHARACTER_0} investigated air resistance using two parachutes A and B. Parachute B took longer to fall (45s) than A (15s). String length and mass were the same for the valid comparison. Which data led to the conclusion that a larger canopy creates more air resistance?
options: (1) Same string, same mass, A has larger canopy but falls faster
(2) Different string, same mass, B falls slower
(3) Same string, same mass, B has larger canopy and falls slower
(4) Same mass, B has larger canopy but different string
correct_answer: (3) Same string, same mass, B has larger canopy and falls slower
explanation: To test the effect of canopy size, all other factors (string length and mass) must be the same. Parachute B took longer to fall (45s vs 15s), proving a larger canopy creates more air resistance.

template: {CHARACTER_0} placed three metal objects A, B and C of the same size at different distances from a light source. A is closest to the light, C is furthest. Which diagram correctly shows the shadows cast on the screen?
options: Shadow A smallest, shadow C largest
Shadow A largest, shadow C smallest
Shadows B and C same size, A smaller
correct_answer: Shadow A largest, shadow C smallest
explanation: Objects closer to the light source cast larger shadows. Shadow A is the largest because it is the closest to the light.

template: {CHARACTER_0} boiled water in three identical pots X, Y and Z of different materials. All pots boiled at the same time despite X being the best heat conductor and Z being the poorest. Why did this happen, and what was the likely volume of water used?
explanation: Since all water boiled at the same time, the best conductor (X) must have had the most water to compensate for faster heating. The poor conductor (Z) could only boil a small amount in that time.
